stopConditions: Stop after verified official University of Glasgow sources support staged student, assessment, academic-integrity, security/privacy, and research claims.
Do not crawl login-only SharePoint or Microsoft Sway staff-only resources.
Do not stage raw HTML, PDFs, screenshots, or full page text in Git.

methodsAttempted.notes: Found student guidance, learning-and-teaching guidance, Regulation 32, research guidance, and official repository PDFs.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl public-web searches restricted to official University of Glasgow domains found multiple official English pages for student AI guidance, GenAI assessment guidance, Regulation 32, Copilot security/privacy guidance, researcher guidance, PGR guidance, and staff guidance. Repository PDFs, webinar listings, and thesis PDFs were rejected because they did not directly state official policy or guidance.

artifacts.notes: Official repository PDF was not used because publication/report artifacts are not binding university policy or guidance evidence.
Official webinar listing was not used because it is event/training metadata rather than direct policy or guidance text.
Official thesis repository PDF was not used because it does not state a University of Glasgow AI policy or guidance position.

artifacts.claimText: University of Glasgow student guidance says the university does not seek to prohibit student AI-tool use generally and instead supports effective, ethical, critical, and transparent use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Glasgow student guidance says using AI or other computational aids in university work without acknowledging the input counts as academic misconduct.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Plagiarism & Academic Integrity Code
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All work submitted by students for assessment is accepted on the understanding that it is the student's own effort. This means students' work should not contain content that has been produced by another person, website, software or Artificial intelligence (AI) tool (except where AI use is explicitly permitted).
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Plagiarism & Academic Integrity Code
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Plagiarism & Academic Integrity Code
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Using any website or generative artificial intelligence (AI) software that generates answers or references is prohibited. In some Schools, AI tools may be used in some circumstances and for specific purposes, but students must not misuse AI tools outwith specific assessment instructions. Any use of AI must be referenced in the work for transparency.

artifacts.sourceTitle: How can I adapt assessment to deal with GenAI?
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is important to recognise that the use of GenAI in assessment should not, in all cases, be treated as plagiarism. Rather we should carefully consider how we design assessment to ensure that students can demonstrate skills beyond purely knowledge recall.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: There is therefore one overriding principle, which applies to all staff, students, and researchers at the University: any use of generative AI tools must be accompanied by critical analysis and oversight on the part of the user.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged candidate because the official student guidance directly says the university does not seek to prohibit AI use and supports ethical, critical, transparent use.
Approved as a narrow staged candidate because the student guidance directly ties unacknowledged AI or computational-aid input to academic misconduct.
Approved as a formal-policy staged candidate because Regulation 32 directly states the assessed-work boundary and explicit-permission exception.
Approved as a narrow staged candidate because Regulation 32 directly states the generated-answer/reference prohibition, School-specific exceptions, and referencing requirement.
Approved as teaching guidance because the official Learning and Teaching page explicitly frames the guidance as University policy for assessment practice and names three scenarios.
Approved with narrow wording that preserves the source boundary: Schools decide locally within the three levels described by central policy.
Approved as assessment-design guidance, not a disciplinary rule, because the source directly says GenAI use should not always be treated as plagiarism.
Approved as security-review guidance because the official IT page directly says Copilot Chat is the only AI service approved for University data and requires UofG credentials.
Approved as a privacy staged candidate because the official IT guidance directly states the confidential/sensitive-information restriction.
Approved as research guidance because the official researcher page directly states the critical-analysis and oversight principle.
Approved as research guidance because the source directly links research AI use to ethical approval and data protection processes.
Approved as PGR privacy guidance because the official checklist directly names confidential and sensitive information, research data, and peer review content.

artifacts.title: University of Glasgow staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for University of Glasgow (QS 2026 rank 79; United Kingdom) found official English-language University of Glasgow sources for student AI-use guidance, academic integrity and Regulation 32, GenAI assessment guidance, local school AI-use levels, Copilot security approval for University data, confidential/sensitive-data restrictions, researcher oversight, ethics/data-protection processes, and PGR confidential-data cautions. All claims are staged candidates only and require human review before canonical publication.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run does not publish canonical claims and does not mark any item human_reviewed.
Staff-only SharePoint and Microsoft Sway resources were not accessed; the staged claims rely only on publicly accessible official University of Glasgow pages.
Rejected official repository PDFs, thesis PDFs, and webinar pages were not used as policy evidence because they did not directly state official AI policy or guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
